title: Knuth's Minrank Problem
statement_text: Determine the asymptotic behavior of the typical $\mathrm{min\text{-}rank}_{\mathbb{R}}(G(n,p))$ for fixed $p \in (0,1)$.
context_text: Knuth raised this problem in 1994 in a survey on the Lov\'asz theta function and orthogonal representations, noting that the minrank is at least $\Omega(\sqrt{n})$. The problem asks for the correct asymptotic order of magnitude of the typical minrank of $G(n,p)$ over the real field.
paper_contribution: Proves that for every field $F = F(n)$ and every $p = p(n)$ satisfying $n^{-1} \leq p \leq 1 - n^{-0.99}$, $\mathrm{min\text{-}rank}_F(G(n,p)) = \Theta\!\left(\frac{n\log(1/p)}{\log n}\right)$ w.h.p., settling the problem in full generality including the real field.
notes: Section 5, described in the paper as containing open problems, is absent from the extracted text; author-stated conjectures or questions from that section may be missing.
paper_title: The Minrank of Random Graphs over Arbitrary Fields
